title: Easy Frame-and-Panel Cabinet Doors
summary: A shop-made frame-and-panel door built entirely with pocket holes, sized by a fixed formula (rails and panel cut 5" smaller than the finished door) so it scales to any cabinet opening.
description:
This is a technique for building your own frame-and-panel doors to any size, rather than a fixed-dimension project — the whole method comes down to one repeatable formula: cut the panel and rails 5" less than your desired finished door dimensions, and cut the stiles to the full finished dimension. Once you know that relationship, you can build a door for almost any cabinet opening using the same steps.

**Getting the plywood panel cut perfectly square and straight matters more than any other single step** — a fixed saw (miter saw or table saw) is strongly preferred, and if you're using a circular saw, run it against a straight edge or rip guide rather than freehand. Drill 1/2" pocket holes around all four sides of the panel, spaced roughly every 8", with at least two holes per side.

Attach the rails to the top and bottom of the panel first, through the panel's pocket holes, keeping the back and all outside edges flush — then drill 3/4" pocket holes on the ends of the rails for the stiles. **The stiles go on last**, using two different screw lengths for the two different pocket hole depths (1" screws for the panel's 1/2" holes, 1-1/4" for the rail-end 3/4" holes) — mixing these up is an easy mistake worth double-checking before driving screws. Fill the pocket holes with plugs or wood filler once assembled.

The worked example below is sized for a 24" x 30" door — a representative size showing the formula in action — but the actual dimensions scale to whatever opening you're building for.

tools.note: Preferred for cutting the panel square and straight.
Alternative for a square, straight panel cut.

materials.name: 1x3 hardwood boards, 2-1/2" wide (poplar for paint grade)
Wood filler or pocket hole plugs

cutList.part: Panel (worked example: 24 in x 30 in finished door)
cutList.note: Formula: cut 5" less than finished door width and height.

cutList.part: Rails (worked example: 24 in x 30 in finished door)
cutList.material: 1x3 hardwood boards, 2-1/2" wide (poplar for paint grade)
cutList.note: Formula: cut 5" less than finished door width.

cutList.part: Stiles (worked example: 24 in x 30 in finished door)
cutList.material: 1x3 hardwood boards, 2-1/2" wide (poplar for paint grade)
cutList.note: Formula: cut to full finished door height.

steps.title: Cut and drill the panel
steps.body: Cut the plywood panel to 5" less than your desired finished door width and height, keeping the cut square and straight. Drill 1/2" pocket holes around all four sides, spaced about every 8", at least two per side.

steps.title: Attach the rails
steps.body: Attach the rails to the top and bottom of the panel through the 1/2" pocket holes with 1" pocket hole screws, keeping the back and all outside edges flush. Drill 3/4" pocket holes on the ends of the rails for the stiles.
materials: 1x3 hardwood boards, 2-1/2" wide (poplar for paint grade)

steps.title: Attach the stiles and finish
steps.body: Attach the stiles to the rails and panel with 1" screws (for the 1/2" pocket holes) and 1-1/4" screws (for the 3/4" pocket holes), keeping all outside edges and the back flush. Fill pocket holes with plugs or wood filler and sand smooth.
materials: 1x3 hardwood boards, 2-1/2" wide (poplar for paint grade)
Wood filler or pocket hole plugs

images.alt: Shop-made frame-and-panel cabinet door built with pocket holes
